params.name: Rutherford Model
params.description:
On Mar 16, 1911 AD, Rutherford officially proposed his atomic model.
Rutherford, a physicist born in New Zealand, developed this model to describe the structure of an atom. His model proposed that the atom consists of a central charge (a positively charged nucleus) surrounded by negatively charged electrons orbiting in a cloud. The stable motion of the atom results from the interaction forces between these charges. This model provided a new perspective on atomic structure research and laid a …
